text: And he said to me: My grace is sufficient for thee: for power is made perfect in infirmity. Gladly therefore will I glory in my infirmities, that the power of Christ may dwell in me.
I will put enmities between thee and the woman, and thy seed and her seed: she shall crush thy head, and thou shalt lie in wait for her heel.
Be nothing solicitous: but in every thing, by prayer and supplication, with thanksgiving, let your petitions be made known to God.
Jesus saith to him: I am the way, and the truth, and the life. No man cometh to the Father, but by me.
For the wages of sin is death. But the grace of God, life everlasting, in Christ Jesus our Lord.

[1] What shall we say then? shall we continue in sin, that grace may abound? [2] God forbid. For we that are dead to sin, how shall we live any longer therein? [3] Know you not that all we, who are baptized in Christ Jesus, are baptized in his death? [4] For we are buried together with him by baptism into death; that as Christ is risen from the dead by the glory of the Father, so we also may walk in newness of life.
In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
This beginning of miracles did Jesus in Cana of Galilee; and manifested his glory, and his disciples believed in him.
And the angel being come in, said unto her: Hail, full of grace, the Lord is with thee: blessed art thou among women.

[41] And it came to pass, that when Elizabeth heard the salutation of Mary, the infant leaped in her womb. And Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Ghost: [42] And she cried out with a loud voice, and said: Blessed art thou among women, and blessed is the fruit of thy womb.
The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom. Fools despise wisdom and instruction.

[1] My son, forget not my law, and let thy heart keep my commandments. [2] For they shall add to thee length of days, and years of life and peace. [3] Let not mercy and truth leave thee, put them about thy neck, and write them in the tables of thy heart: [4] And thou shalt find grace and good understanding before God and men. [5] Have confidence in the Lord with all thy heart, and lean not upon thy own prudence.
When I called upon him, the God of my justice heard me: when I was in distress, thou hast enlarged me. Have mercy on me: and hear my prayer.
For God so loved the world, as to give his only begotten Son; that whosoever believeth in him, may not perish, but may have life everlasting.
Therefore I say unto you, all things, whatsoever you ask when ye pray, believe that you shall receive; and they shall come unto you.
But the souls of the just are in the hand of God, and the torment of death shall not touch them.
But grow in grace, and in the knowledge of our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ. To him be glory both now and unto the day of eternity. Amen.
Because he hath regarded the humility of his handmaid; for behold from henceforth all generations shall call me blessed.
For wisdom is better than all the most precious things: and whatsoever may be desired cannot be compared to it.
I can do all things in him who strengtheneth me.
And grieve not the holy Spirit of God: whereby you are sealed unto the day of redemption.
And be ye kind one to another; merciful, forgiving one another, even as God hath forgiven you in Christ.
He shall scorn the scorners, and to the meek he will give grace.
And there shall be life to thy soul, and grace to thy mouth.
Well ordered words are as a honeycomb: sweet to the soul, and health to the bones.
He that followeth justice and mercy, shall find life, justice, and glory.
They that trust in him, shall understand the truth: and they that are faithful in love shall rest in him: for grace and peace is to his elect.
For there is a shame that bringeth sin, and there is a shame that bringeth glory and grace.
I will praise thee: because thou hast heard me, and art become my salvation.
Cast thy care upon the Lord, and he shall sustain thee: he shall not suffer the just to waver for ever.
Blessed are the poor in spirit: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
Blessed are the clean of heart: for they shall see God.
But he giveth greater grace. Wherefore he saith: God resisteth the proud, and giveth grace to the humble.
For God loveth mercy and truth: the Lord will give grace and glory.
The beginning of wisdom, get wisdom, and with all thy possession purchase prudence.
But the Paraclete, the Holy Ghost, whom the Father will send in my name, he will teach you all things, and bring all things to your mind, whatsoever I shall have said to you.
But you shall receive the power of the Holy Ghost coming upon you, and you shall be witnesses unto me in Jerusalem, and in all Judea, and Samaria, and even to the uttermost part of the earth.
And hope confoundeth not: because the charity of God is poured forth in our hearts, by the Holy Ghost, who is given to us.
Or know you not, that your members are the temple of the Holy Ghost, who is in you, whom you have from God; and you are not your own?
If you then, being evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father from heaven give the good Spirit to them that ask him?
